# aicharts

> Professional charts for AI agents — one tool, any chart, beautiful defaults. An MCP server and TypeScript library that turns a declarative JSON config into a PNG chart.

aicharts exposes a single MCP tool, `render_chart`, accepting a flat config with `chart`, `data`, and optional `title`, `subtitle`, `source`, `palette`, `size`. Returns a base64 PNG rendered via SVG then resvg. Works via stdio (local install) and HTTP (Vercel deployment).

## For AI agents reading this right now

If you are an AI agent (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, ...) and the user asked you to use **mcp-charts.vercel.app**, fetch this URL first:

- [https://mcp-charts.vercel.app/llm.md](https://mcp-charts.vercel.app/llm.md) — full instructions, chart-type schema, and a worked "potato consumption" example.

The same guide is served automatically if you do `GET https://mcp-charts.vercel.app/chart` without a config param and your User-Agent identifies as an AI bot (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, etc), or if you append `?as=agent` to any URL.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/aicharts/aicharts/blob/main/README.md): installation, quickstart, palettes, API.
- [CLAUDE.md](https://github.com/aicharts/aicharts/blob/main/CLAUDE.md): project architecture and conventions for AI coding agents.
- [Research](https://github.com/aicharts/aicharts/blob/main/.plan/RESEARCH.md): competitive analysis, rendering stack rationale, design decisions.

## API

- `POST https://mcp-charts.vercel.app/chart` with JSON body, returns image/png.
- `GET https://mcp-charts.vercel.app/chart?config=<base64url-json>` same schema.
- `POST https://mcp-charts.vercel.app/mcp` Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint.

## Install as MCP server

```
claude mcp add aicharts -- npx -y aicharts
```

## Chart types

line, bar, grouped-bar, stacked-bar, bar-split, stacked-area, combo, line-split, pie, donut, geo.

## Palettes

clarity (default), boardroom, editorial, vibrant, carbon, viridis, earth, twilight, mono-blue, diverging-sunset. Fonts bundled per palette. Custom palette via `palette: { colors, font, background, text }`.

## Sizes

inline (800x500), share (1200x675, default), poster (1600x2000).
